5.0.2.summary: A maintenance release — various enhancements and critical fixes across Kubernetes management and carrier-grade network function orchestration.
highlights: Calico add-on update for Workload Clusters on Kubernetes 1.27, 1.28, 1.29, and 1.30 — deployed automatically as part of the TCA 3.3.0.1 upgrade, even if your Kubernetes version doesn't change; may cause a brief service fluctuation, recommended since it contains Calico fixes.

relatedReleases.title: New in Telco Cloud Automation 3.3.0.1
highlights: More VMX configuration options per workload cluster, for finer control over node placement and performance.
Clearer error messages when a workload cluster rehome operation fails, making it easier to identify and fix the cause.
System service logs can now be forwarded to a journal log server, making them easier to retrieve.
Photon 5 Kubernetes 1.30.2 BYOI template updated with security fixes and cgroup v2 support.
Workload clusters running an older Whereabouts add-on are now upgraded automatically as part of the Kubernetes upgrade — no separate step needed.
Cluster rehome operations now validate upfront that the source and target management clusters meet the rehoming prerequisites, before the operation proceeds.
Kafka health checks now reconnect automatically when Kafka becomes unhealthy, instead of requiring manual intervention.
Clearer error messages when a management cluster's Kubernetes upgrade fails, making it easier to identify and fix the cause.
relatedReleases.releaseNotesLabel: VMware Telco Cloud Automation 3.3.0.1 Release Notes

5.1.summary: VMware Telco Cloud Platform 5.1 is a significant milestone release, featuring comprehensive automation enhancements for both Kubernetes management and carrier-grade network function orchestration.

whyUpgrade.title: Lifecycle Management (LCM)
whyUpgrade.text: Introduces Kubernetes version 1.33 with an additional year of support, and adds automated upgrade workflows for both Kubernetes clusters and the underlying ESXi infrastructure — simplifying lifecycle management and significantly reducing operational overhead.

whyUpgrade.title: Security
whyUpgrade.text: Adds fleet-wide security policy enforcement, automatic certificate monitoring and management, and stronger Harbor security capabilities — strengthening security and safeguarding data center integrity.

whyUpgrade.title: CNF Automation
whyUpgrade.text: Supports Git-based artifact management for CNF catalogs, improving automation and operational efficiency.

whyUpgrade.title: Troubleshooting
whyUpgrade.text: Introduces a new command-line tool for faster log collection and troubleshooting, helping both on-site Operations and Broadcom Support accelerate root-cause analysis.

categories.title: Carrier-Grade Kubernetes Infrastructure
items: Kubernetes 1.33.1 support (Tanzu Kubernetes Grid 2.5.4) — cuts the lag between upstream Kubernetes releases and their integration into TCP to about 3 months, giving quicker access to the latest features and fixes. It also carries an extra year of support (2 years total), a benefit that now also applies retroactively to Kubernetes 1.30.2 and 1.27.15.

Manage 7 different Kubernetes versions at once through a single Tanzu Kubernetes management cluster and a single TCA instance, so network functions can each run on the version they actually need instead of the fleet being forced onto one version.
Avi Load Balancer 30.2.3 adds IPv6 support between AKO and the Avi controller, so CNFs can use ingress virtual IPs over IPv4, IPv6, or dual-stack networks.

categories.title: Carrier-Grade VNF and CNF Automation and Orchestration
items: CaaS ES Upgrade Workflow: single-click automated Kubernetes upgrades with enhanced fail-fast conditions, reducing downtime and operational overhead, and giving flexibility to manage multiple Kubernetes versions across TCP releases based on network function requirements.
Rehoming Workflow: move workload clusters between management clusters in batches, enabling non-disruptive cluster maintenance.
IaaS LCM Workflow: upgrade ESXi clusters hosting SR-IOV and latency-sensitive workloads directly through TCA.
A new CLI command for on-demand Kubernetes cluster/node log generation, independent of the standard TCA log bundle, for faster root-cause analysis with less operational overhead.

Certificate Observability and Auto-Renewal: automatic monitoring and renewal of cluster component certificates and kubeconfig tokens, cutting manual effort and reducing the risk of service interruptions from expired certificates.

Harbor Certificate and Credential Management: TCA alerts on Harbor certificate/credential issues before they cause problems, and lets admins push a single-click update cascaded to every TCA-managed cluster — simplifying credential management and speeding up root-cause analysis.

Kubernetes Policy Management: pre-configured, NSA/CISA-aligned security policies applied fleet-wide with centralized violation tracking and automatic drift reconciliation, helping CSPs meet compliance requirements and support security audits.
Git-based CSAR management: version-controlled Network Function packages via an external Git repository, so the same package stays consistent and reusable across TCA deployments.
Harbor — IPv6 and dual-stack deployment support for CNF workload clusters.
Harbor — audit logging of key events, aligned to UK Telecommunications Security Act (TSA) compliance requirements.
Harbor — automatic SBOM generation per artifact, for visibility into dependencies, versions, and licenses to help manage security issues.
Harbor — Trivy database details viewable directly in the TCA UI, without leaving the workflow.
Harbor — Pod Security Admission support, letting Harbor pods run with non-privileged access.

relatedReleases.title: New in Telco Cloud Automation 3.4
highlights: Audit Log: TCA events are now organized by category and severity, and can be filtered and searched on those classifications.
Airgap Server: upgraded to Photon 5 (in-place upgrade supported from Photon 4), Harbor upgraded to 2.13.1, and support for OCI-format Helm chart uploads.
Automated log collection from TKG clusters and cluster nodes, directly from TCA-CP — for faster troubleshooting.
Simplified TCA-CP/TCA-M pairing, with the whole TCA infrastructure now managed from a single pane.
CNF inventory now loads on-demand for better performance, with warnings when Helm chart configurations grow too large, and support for hardcoded image pull secrets in Helm charts.
TCA appliance: session management (view/terminate open TCA-M sessions), upgraded to Photon 5, and internal Kubernetes upgraded for security.
OpenID Connect (OIDC) can now be configured as an authentication provider alongside vCenter and Active Directory — a limited-availability feature, not enabled by default.
Telco Cloud Platform Management Pack for Aria Operations: a dashboard for cluster certificate status, with alerts when something needs attention.
relatedReleases.releaseNotesLabel: VMware Telco Cloud Automation 3.4 Release Notes
